lines.english: Why was the first tunnel not completed?
lines.chinese: 为什么第一条隧道没有建成？
lines.raw: Why was the first tunnel not completed? | 为什么第一条隧道没有建成？

lines.english: In 1858, a French engineer, Aime Thome de Gamond,
lines.chinese: 1858年，一位名叫埃梅·托梅·德·加蒙的法国工程师，
lines.raw: In 1858, a French engineer, Aime Thome de Gamond, | 1858年，一位名叫埃梅·托梅·德·加蒙的法国工程师，

lines.english: arrived in England with a plan for a 21-mile tunnel under the English Channel.
lines.chinese: 带着修建一条21英里长的英吉利海峡隧道的计划抵达英国。
lines.raw: arrived in England with a plan for a 21-mile tunnel under the English Channel. | 带着修建一条21英里长的英吉利海峡隧道的计划抵达英国。

lines.english: He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the centre of the Channel.
lines.chinese: 他说可以在海峡中央修建一个平台。
lines.raw: He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the centre of the Channel. | 他说可以在海峡中央修建一个平台。

lines.english: This platform would serve as a port and a railway station.
lines.chinese: 这个平台将作为港口和火车站。
lines.raw: This platform would serve as a port and a railway station. | 这个平台将作为港口和火车站。

lines.english: The tunnel would be well-ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level.
lines.chinese: 如果在海平面以上修建高烟囱，隧道将能很好地通风。
lines.raw: The tunnel would be well-ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level. | 如果在海平面以上修建高烟囱，隧道将能很好地通风。

lines.english: In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Low.
lines.chinese: 1860年，英国人威廉·洛提出了一个更好的方案。
lines.raw: In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Low. | 1860年，英国人威廉·洛提出了一个更好的方案。

lines.english: He suggested that a double railway-tunnel should be built.
lines.chinese: 他建议修建一条双轨铁路隧道。
lines.raw: He suggested that a double railway-tunnel should be built. | 他建议修建一条双轨铁路隧道。

lines.english: This would solve the problem of ventilation for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it.
lines.chinese: 这将解决通风问题，因为如果火车进入这条隧道，它会从后面吸入新鲜空气。
lines.raw: This would solve the problem of ventilation for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it. | 这将解决通风问题，因为如果火车进入这条隧道，它会从后面吸入新鲜空气。

lines.english: Forty-two years later a tunnel was actually begun.
lines.chinese: 四十二年后，一条隧道实际开始动工。
lines.raw: Forty-two years later a tunnel was actually begun. | 四十二年后，一条隧道实际开始动工。

lines.english: If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed.
lines.chinese: 如果当时英国人没有害怕入侵，它就会建成。
lines.raw: If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed. | 如果当时英国人没有害怕入侵，它就会建成。

lines.english: The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel.
lines.chinese: 世界不得不又等了将近100年才等到英吉利海峡隧道。
lines.raw: The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel. | 世界不得不又等了将近100年才等到英吉利海峡隧道。

lines.english: It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent.
lines.chinese: 它于1994年3月7日正式开通，最终将英国与欧洲大陆连接起来。
lines.raw: It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent. | 它于1994年3月7日正式开通，最终将英国与欧洲大陆连接起来。
